rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GENETTE TRAMMELL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Respondent Kilby Stenkamp To Compel Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents; Request For Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: [1 of 2] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Joseph M. Quinn. Respondents Kilby Stenkamp and Vanguard Properties, Inc.'s Motion to Compel Responses to Request for Production of Documents is DENIED. If responding party fails to respond, all that need be shown in the moving papers is that the document demand was properly served on the opposing party, that the time to respond has expired, and that no response of any kind has been served. See Leach v. Sup. Ct. (Markum) (1980) 111 Cal.App.3d 902, 905-906. No separate statement is required and the moving party is not required to show a reasonable and good faith attempt to resolve the matter informally with opposing counsel. CCP Section 2030.290; Sinaiko Healthcare Consulting, Inc. v. Pacific Healthcare Consultants (2007) 148 Cal.App.4th 411. Here, Stenkamp and Vanguard have not shown that their document demand was properly served. The basic element of service is set out in CCP Section 2031.040: "The party making a demand  shall serve a copy of the demand on the party to whom it is directed and on all other parties who have appeared in the action." No Proof of Service has been presented to evidence service on Lawrence Porchia or Christiane Hipps, Porchia's attorney of record on January 19, 2021. The Proof of Service attached to Stenkamp and Vanguard's idemand stated that electronic service was made on January 19, 2021, to Keystone Law Group, Inc. Keystone Law Group, Inc. ceased representing Porchia in June of 2019. Stenkamp and Vanguard state in their moving papers that Porchia was served again on March 9, 2021; but, no Proof of Service has been presented to support the claim, and in any event the moving parties don't claim that they served Porchia's then counsel, Ms. Hipps, or properly served Porchia. It is not enough for Stenkamp and Vanguard to show that Porchia received a copy of the demand; a moving party must establish "proper service," and Stenkamp and Vanguard have failed to do so. Stenkamp and Vanguard cite Thatcher v. Lucky Stores, Inc. (2000) 79 Cal.App.4th 1081 and Sexton v. Sup. Ct. (1997) 58 Cal.App.4th 1403 for the proposition that a failure to oppose a motion is an admission that the motion is meritorious and, thus, the party adversely affected by the motion may not present oral argument at the hearing and the court is duty-bound to grant the motion. Nonsense. The moving party still must bear its burden. Thatcher makes this plain. There, the appellate court voided the local rule at issue (and effectively relied upon by moving parties here) to the extent it invited trial courts to grant motions simply for lack of opposition, regardless whether the moving part satisfied their initial burden of proof. See Thatcher, supra, 79 Cal.App.4th at pp. 1086-1087. Here, Stenkamp and Vanguard had the initial burden to prove proper service of the form interrogatories on Porchia and they failed to carry this burden. The motion, thus, fails, notwithstanding the absence of a formal opposition.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GENETTE TRAMMELL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Respondent Kilby Stenkamp To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories; Request For Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: [1 of 2] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Joseph M. Quinn. Respondents Kilby Stenkamp and Vanguard Properties, Inc.'s Motion to Compel Responses to Form Interrogatories is DENIED. If responding party fails to respond, all that need be shown in the moving papers is that a set of interrogatories was properly served on the opposing party, that the time to respond has expired, and that no response of any kind has been served. See Leach v. Sup. Ct. (Markum) (1980) 111 Cal.App.3d 902, 905-906. No separate statement is required and the moving party is not required to show a reasonable and good faith attempt to resolve the matter informally with opposing counsel. CCP Section 2030.290; Sinaiko Healthcare Consulting, Inc. v. Pacific Healthcare Consultants (2007) 148 Cal.App.4th 411. Here, Stenkamp and Vanguard have not shown that their form interrogatories were properly served. The basic element of service is set out in CCP Section 2030.080(a): "The party propounding interrogatories shall serve a copy of them on the party to whom the interrogatories are directed." No Proof of Service has been presented to evidence service on Lawrence Porchia or Christiane Hipps, Porchia's attorney of record on January 19, 2021. The Proof of Service attached to Stenkamp and Vanguard's interrogatories stated that electronic service was made on January 19, 2021, to Keystone Law Group, Inc. Keystone Law Group, Inc. ceased representing Porchia in June of 2019. Stenkamp and Vanguard state in their moving papers that Porchia was served again on March 9, 2021; but, no Proof of Service has been presented to support the claim, and in any event the moving parties don't claim that they served Porchia's then counsel, Ms. Hipps, or properly served Porchia. It is not enough for Stenkamp and Vanguard to show that Porchia received a copy of the form interrogatories; a moving party must establish "proper service," and Stenkamp and Vanguard have failed to do so. Stenkamp and Vanguard cite Thatcher v. Lucky Stores, Inc. (2000) 79 Cal.App.4th 1081 and Sexton v. Sup. Ct. (1997) 58 Cal.App.4th 1403 for the proposition that a failure to oppose a motion is an admission that the motion is meritorious and, thus, the party adversely affected by the motion may not present oral argument at the hearing and the court is duty-bound to grant the motion. Nonsense. The moving party still must bear its burden. Thatcher makes this plain. There, the appellate court voided the local rule at issue (and effectively relied upon by moving parties here) to the extent it invited trial courts to grant motions simply for lack of opposition, regardless whether the moving party satisfied their initial burden of proof. See Thatcher, supra, 79 Cal.App.4th at pp. 1086-1087. Here, Stenkamp and Vanguard had the initial burden to prove proper service of the form interrogatories on Porchia and they failed to carry this burden. The motion, thus, fails, notwithstanding the absence of a formal opposition.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GENETTE TRAMMELL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion Of Respondent Kilby Stenkamp To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories; Request For Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities (Added For Posting Of The Tentative Ruling
rulings.Rulings: [2 of 2] Stenkamp and Vanguard's request for sanctions is DENIED. Stenkamp and Vanguard have not successfully made a motion to compel under CCP Section 2030.300(d). Stenkamp and Vanguard shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. Petitioner is ordered to lodge with the clerk in Department 204 a proposed order consistent with this tentative by the time set for this hearing. Remote appearance is available in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE CLAUDELL & MARY LEBLANC 2004 TRUST ESTABLISHED SEPTEMBER 30, 2004
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Determine Validity Of Trust Provision
rulings.Rulings: continued to 12/20/21. an amendment to the petition must be filed that includes a full copy of the original trust. in addition, paragraph 7 must be revised to identify the status of the persons listed (trust beneficiaries v. heirs, or both). no later than noon on 12/10/21, counsel must provide a file stamped hard copy of the petition, the amendment and a proposed order to the examiner in room 202. please note, examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DON MICHAEL WISDOM
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Matter will be called to admit will. No appearance is required.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MARY M. GANTT REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST ESTABLISHED TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Declare The Property Of The Estate Of Mary M. Gantt Included In The Mary M. Gantt Revocable Living Trust Established On April 24, 2017
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/13/21. No courtesy copy of the petition has been received yet by the assigned Examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and C.] No proposed order. Proposed orders should not be E-filed. [LRSF 14.7 A.] No proof of service has been filed. Courtesy copies of the petition and proof of service and the proposed order must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 12/3/21.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LEE B. GOMES
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/07/2021. 1) The following people are mentioned in Decedent's Will and are not included on page 4 under #8 and must be included even if deceased and must include their date of death: Scott Brian Gomes, Jon Alexander Gomes, Gwenette Higa, Gwendolyn Louella Gomes. 2) Petition page 4 #8 must include the relationship of each person to Decedent. 3) Please explain who is now the Trustee of the Lee B. Gomes Family Trust. 4) Please explain why on page 2 #2.e. petitioner checked Special administrator is the named executor, and the will waives bond. There is no special administrator in this estate. 5) Petitioner must file proof of publication. 6) Petitioner must give 15 days' notice of the petition on DE-121. 7) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form.[LRSF 14.18 E.] An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 11/22/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
